feed.title: Post-Quantum BIP-86 Recovery via zk-STARK Proof of BIP-32 Seed Knowledge

entry.title: Post-Quantum BIP-86 Recovery via zk-STARK Proof of BIP-32 Seed Knowledge
entry.summary:
The recent discussion on the Bitcoin Development Mailing List highlights an innovative approach to blockchain verification and recovery schemes. The focus is on a specific demonstration that shows the feasibility of including a single 200 KB proof per block, which challenges some common misconceptions about the capabilities of current blockchain technology. This feature is not intended to replace Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) signatures but serves as a reactive soft fork—a rescue scheme aimed at allowing coin owners to reclaim their assets should they not transition in time to future proactive forks that integrate new signature mechanisms.
Verification using STARKs (Scalable Transparent ARguments of Knowledge) is addressed, emphasizing its efficiency with verification times cut down to tens of milliseconds. This contradicts the belief that STARKs require multiple seconds for verification, a point substantiated by executable code available in a linked repository, which demonstrates these capabilities in real-time application scenarios ([repository link](URL)).
Furthermore, the message clarifies that the proposed architecture is not set for adoption as-is, but rather as a conceptual prototype. If serious consideration were given to this approach, it would likely involve a customized circuit tailored to specific security claims related to the blockchain. As demonstrated, even simple claims could potentially be verified with just a few hash function invocations, showcasing the potential for streamlined and efficient processes within blockchain operations. This information underscores ongoing discussions and developments in blockchain technology, particularly in terms of security and verification processes.
